speaker: Lynette
content: boys, I am not gonna tell you again, sit. I mean it, I am serious. You guys are gonna be in so much trouble if you don't sit back in those seats.
mark: I mean it 我是认真的
be in trouble 处于某种麻烦的状态中 get (into) trouble 招致、得到麻烦
sit back 坐回

speaker: Alice
content: For the first time, Lynette could see this was not the innocent play of children, she was being challenged. So she decided it was time to get creative.
mark: For the first time 第一次
innocent adj. 天真的，幼稚的；清白的，无罪的；
she was being challenged 过去进行时的被动语态
it was time to do sth. 是做某事的时候了，time表示时候；时机时是一种泛指的概念，不需要加the 来特指
get creative 发挥创造力，尝试新的想法或方法。

speaker: Lynette
content: Out! You can't behave? You heard me, you cannot ride. you, out, move it.
mark: 你不听话是吧？我说得很清楚 —— 你就不能坐（这车）。
You can’t behave? 你不能守规矩 / 你不听话？
you heard me 我说得很清楚、你听见了（强调语气）

speaker: Children
content: Where is she going?

speaker: Lynette
content: OK, mommy is a genius, five, six, all right. Uh, mommy will be right back.
mark: genius n. 天赋，天资；天才

speaker: Lynette
content: Boys? If you're hiding, you've got to stop now, cause mommy doesn't think it's funny.
mark: cause n. 原因；事业；理由 v. 引起 == because的口语化、非正式写法

speaker: Lynette
content: Oh, excuse me. I'm sorry, I'm looking for my boys, three boys with red hair, have you seen them?

speaker: Lady
content: Yeah, I also saw you drive away and leave them.

speaker: Lynette
content: I know，I was just trying to scare them into behaving, did you see where they went?
mark: scare sb. into doing sth. 吓唬某人使其做某事 scare sb into behaving  吓唬某人使其守规矩

speaker: Lady
content: Yeah, they're in my kitchen, listen, it seems to me that you have some anger management issues.
mark: it seems to me that...用于表达个人的观点和看法，在我看来……；我觉得……
it seems to be 后面一般接名词、形容词或不定式短语等，而不接that引导的从句
anger management 愤怒管理，情绪控制

speaker: Lynette
content: I have four kids under the age of six, I absolutely have anger management issues.

speaker: Lady
content: I think you need to talk to somebody because abandoning your children.

speaker: Lynette
content: I didn't abandon them, I came back.

speaker: Lady
content: I'm just saying it's not normal.
mark: I'm just saying 我只是说一下：用于强调自己只是提供意见或建议，而不是要求别人采取行动或做出决定。

speaker: Lynette
content: Well. my kids aren't normal, and now I don't have time for this, lady. So, boys, get out here.

speaker: Lady
content: I don't think they should go anywhere until you calm down.
mark: calm down 冷静下来

speaker: Lynette
content: Get in the car.

speaker: Children
content: She's got cookies.
mark: She's got cookies.她有饼干have got在口语中很常用，表示拥有==have

speaker: Lynette
content: Well, bring them, we're leaving.

speaker: Lady
content: No, stay put.
mark: stay put 不动；待在原地

speaker: Lynette
content: Oh, I don't think so.

speaker: Lady
content: Listen, lady, you and I are gonna talk.

speaker: Children
content: Leave my mommy alone.
mark: leave sb alone 不要打扰某人：不去干扰、改变或触碰某人。

speaker: Lynette
content: Let go of me. What's the matter with you? Run, boys, run! Come on! Oh, crap.
mark: let go of me 放开我，松手
What's the matter with you? 你怎么了？==what’s wrong with you?

speaker: Lynette
content: Boys, you're gonna need to put on your...

speaker: Lady
content: You get back here. Get back or I'm gonna call social services.
mark: social services 社会服务
